output.code:
# Let's open the uploaded text file and read it
path = "/mnt/data/file-MRTFLkTYpecr6uJFDKLH95-Q131.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:4000]

import re

# Find double-quoted substrings
double_quotes = [(m.start(), m.end(), m.group(0)) for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text)]
len(double_quotes), double_quotes[:10]

bracket_quotes = [(m.start(), m.end(), m.group(0)) for m in re.finditer(r'﴿([^﴿﴾]+)﴾', text)]
bracket_quotes

phrase = "لا تجعلوا بيوتكم مقابر، إن الشيطان ينفر من البيت الذي تقرأ فيه سورة البقرة."
idx = text.find(phrase)
idx

phrase2 = "وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ"
idx2 = text.find(phrase2)
idx2, idx2+len(phrase2), text[idx2:idx2+len(phrase2)]

# Verify inner substring for the first double-quoted citation
dq = double_quotes[0]
start, end, encl = dq
inner = text[start+1:end-1]
encl, inner, encl[1:-1] == inner

queries: فَكَيْفَ تَتَّقُونَ إِن كَفَرْتُمْ يَوْمًا يَجْعَلُ الولدان شِيباً سورة المزمل 17

وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ المائدة 23
اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِن ضَعْفٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفًا وَشَيْبَةً الروم 54
وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ المائدة 23

action.query: لا تجعلوا بيوتكم مقابر إن الشيطان ينفر من البيت الذي تقرأ فيه سورة البقرة أبو هريرة صحيح مسلم

output.code:
import re, json

text_path = "/mnt/data/file-MRTFLkTYpecr6uJFDKLH95-Q131.txt"
with open(text_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    txt = f.read()

# Helper to extract inner span from double-quoted
dq_matches = list(re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', txt))
bq_matches = list(re.finditer(r'﴿([^﴿﴾]+)﴾', txt))

# Build items we care about
items = []

# 1) Double-quoted entries to include that are Quran or hadith
# We'll map by their text to type
include_texts = {
    # Quran
    "فَكَيْفَ تَتَّقُونَ إِن كَفَرْتُمْ يَوْمًا يَجْعَلُ الولدان شِيباً": "Aya",
    "الله الذي خلقكم من ضعف ثم جعل من بعد ضعف قوة ثم جعل من بعد قوة ضعفًا وشيبة يخلق ما يشاء وهو العليم القدير": "Aya",
    "وجاءكم النذير": "Aya",
    # Hadith
    "شَيَّبَتْنِي هُودٌ، وَالْوَاقِعَةُ، وَالْمُرْسَلاَتُ، وَعَمَّ يَتَسَاءَلُونَ، وَإِذَا الشَّمْسُ كُوِّرَتْ": "Hadeeth"
}

for m in dq_matches:
    content = m.group(1)
    if content in include_texts:
        items.append({
            "span_start": m.start()+1,
            "span_end": m.end()-1,
            "span_text": content,
            "citation_type": include_texts[content]
        })

# 2) Bracketed Quran ayahs in the text: include verse texts, ignore ones that only include sura:ayah
for m in bq_matches:
    inner = m.group(1).strip()
    # Exclude ones that are just references like "المائدة: ٢٣"
    if ":" in inner and all(ch.isdigit() or ch in " ١٢٣٤٥٦٧٨٩٠:المائدةالنساءآلالعمرانالبقرةالرومالتحريمفاطرق" for ch in inner.replace("سورة","").replace(" ","")):
        continue
    items.append({
        "span_start": m.start()+1,
        "span_end": m.end()-1,
        "span_text": inner,
        "citation_type": "Aya"
    })

# 3) Hadith phrase without quotes
hadith_phrase = "لا تجعلوا بيوتكم مقابر، إن الشيطان ينفر من البيت الذي تقرأ فيه سورة البقرة."
idx = txt.find(hadith_phrase)
if idx != -1:
    items.append({
        "span_start": idx,
        "span_end": idx + len(hadith_phrase),
        "span_text": hadith_phrase,
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth"
    })

# 4) Qur'an phrase without brackets: "وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ"
q_phrase = "وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ"
idx2 = txt.find(q_phrase)
if idx2 != -1:
    items.append({
        "span_start": idx2,
        "span_end": idx2 + len(q_phrase),
        "span_text": q_phrase,
        "citation_type": "Aya"
    })

# Sort items by span_start
items_sorted = sorted(items, key=lambda x: x["span_start"])
len(items_sorted), items_sorted[:5]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
